name: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard)
creator: Joan Daemen and Vincent Rijmen
breakability: No practical attack known. Brute-force infeasible.
modern_use: TLS/HTTPS, disk encryption, VPNs, Wi-Fi (WPA2/WPA3), file encryption
description: AES is the gold-standard symmetric block cipher, selected by NIST in 2001 after a 5-year competition. Operates on 128-bit blocks with 128/192/256-bit keys. GCM mode adds authenticated encryption protecting both confidentiality and integrity.
notes: Use GCM or EAX for authenticated encryption. Never reuse nonces with the same key. AES-256-GCM is the recommended default.
did_you_know: AES replaced DES in 2001 and is estimated to be used in over 90% of internet TLS connections today.
